rows.description: Computus Thomas Stinton Coll. Exon. Rectoris a 2do Nov: 1795 ad eundem diem revolution 1796.
rows.notes: Top of page title is legible though a few letters (e.g. 'Stinton' / 'Exon.') are slightly stylised. No monetary amounts in this line; clear structural header.
Centered section heading. Handwriting of this word is somewhat ornate and ambiguous in letters but clearly a heading introducing the list; no amounts present.
Amounts align to the right-hand £ s d columns very clearly as 152.17.0. Columns are unambiguous and values are within valid ranges.

rows.description: Guinear Rectory half a year to mid
rows.notes: Amount reads clearly as 30.0.0. The place name 'Guinear' / the trailing words are somewhat uncertain in spelling, but the monetary column is well aligned and unambiguous.
Pounds and shillings are clear as 17.2. Pence reads as '10' with a small superscript/mark interpreted as three farthings (¾). Column alignment consistent with totals row.
Amount 8.11.6 is clear and neatly aligned. Description is legible as 'Garsington Sadler'.
Second Garsington line mirrors previous format; monetary 8.11.6 is clear and valid. Surname reading 'Puckett' is confident.
Amount 41.13.10 is clearly written and aligned. The place name 'Tintonshul' / 'Tintonhul' is slightly variant in spelling but readable; no ambiguity in numbers.
Amount reads as 5.1.11 with a small 'ob' style loop after the pence interpreted as halfpenny (0.5). Pounds/shillings alignment is clear.
Monetary figure reads as 2.0.0; the place/name 'Montacute Alast Smiths' is somewhat cramped and letters uncertain, but numbers are legible and in correct columns.
Amount appears as 7.16.2; the surname 'Finy' (or 'Finny') is slightly ambiguous but the numeric columns are clear.

rows.description: South Newington Tenement Tho: Penn 1 year
rows.notes: Description 'Tho: Penn' is readable as abbreviation for Thomas Penn. Amount 2.11.3 lines up in the columns; digits legible though ink is a little lighter.
Amount clearly 40.0.0. 'Menherst' reading for the rectory is probable though historical spelling varies; monetary columns are unambiguous.
Monetary entry 11.14.8 is clear. 'Chase Hill' is legible; slight ink wobble on last digit of pence but alignment confirms reading of '8'.
Amount reads as 12.2.6 but there is some light smearing under the shillings; names 'Barrett and Burgess' are inferred from abbreviated handwriting and partly faded letters.
Handwriting of place name is uncertain ('Truttlewyn' / 'Tuttwyn'); numeric column shows '9.0.0' clearly to the right, but association to the exact text is moderately uncertain due to cramped script.

This Pad Lane line has a small amount in the pounds column read as '1.0.0'. The name 'Conyers' is legible but the first digit is small; column alignment and the later bracket subtotal support reading of 1 pound.
Clear monetary 13.6.8 aligned in the £ s d columns. 'Wilson' reading is neat and unambiguous.

rows.description: Pad Lane Hallifax late Warrel 2 years
rows.notes: Amount 3.6.8 is clear. Surname reads as 'Hallifax' (and 'late Warrel' or similar) — letters are somewhat cramped but plausible; numbers align well.

A bracketed subtotal is written to the right of the three Pad Lane lines as 17.13.4. This matches the sum of the preceding Pad Lane numeric entries (1.0.0 + 13.6.8 + 3.6.8 = 17.13.4). The subtotal is clearly aligned and labelled by the bracket.
Amount 6.0.0 is clear and neatly placed in the £ s d columns. 'Bampton' is legible in the description.
Monetary entry 6.10.10 is clear. The initials and surname 'S. G. Page Turner' are legible; numbers align well with ruling lines.
Amount reads as 0.10.0 (ten shillings). 'Jesus College' text is clear and amount is unambiguous in the shillings column.
Amount appears as 6.0.0. The place name is read as 'Back Gate Coppins' (letters slightly indistinct) but the monetary digits and column placement are clear.
Entry reads as 6.6.0. The place name may be 'Wild Arish' or variant ('Willd Arish'); letters are slightly stylised but the numbers are legible.

Final total at bottom right reads clearly as 392.3.5¼ (5 and a quarter pence). The quarter-penny mark is visible and interpreted as 0.25. Vertical alignment with the £ s d columns is consistent and unambiguous.
